title: Code Review: schemar
text: 217 existing tests pass. issues focus places runtime behavior diverges documented intent reasonable user expect.

what: 1. sch_factor() never validates factor levels (strict mode)
title: Code Review: schemar
text: Severity: High strict = TRUE (default), type check simply .factor(x). Factor levels never compared schema. factor completely wrong levels, extra levels, silently passes validation. check function (schema.R ≈ line 584) verify factor’s levels match (subset ) schema’s levels. Ironically, strict = FALSE path validate values levels (character vectors), making non-strict path stricter strict mode regard. Files: R/schema.R (type_fnsfactorfactorcheck)
code: schema <- sch_schema(x = sch_factor(levels = c("a", "b")))  # Wrong levels — passes: sch_validate(schema, data.frame(x = factor(c("x","y"), levels = c("x","y"))))  # Extra levels — passes: sch_validate(schema, data.frame(x = factor(c("a","b"), levels = c("a","b","EXTRA"))))

what: 2. Non-strict factor rejects NAs even when missing = TRUE
title: Code Review: schemar
text: Severity: High non-strict factor check uses (x %% type$levels). R, NA %% c("","b") returns FALSE, NA causes type check fail — separate missing-value check ever runs. fix add na.rm logic: e.g. (x %% type$levels | .na(x)) (x[!.na(x)] %% type$levels). Files: R/schema.R (type_fnsfactorfactorcheck)
code: schema <- sch_schema(x = sch_factor(levels = c("a","b"), strict = FALSE)) df <- data.frame(x = c("a", NA), stringsAsFactors = FALSE) sch_validate(schema, df) # Error: wrong type — but missing=TRUE should allow NAs

what: 3. check = "names" does not detect missing required columns
title: Code Review: schemar
text: Severity: High documentation check parameter states: "names": check missing required columns unexpected extra columns. validate_names() checks extra columns. Missing required column detection handled inside validate_types_missing(), corresponds check = "types". check = "names" silently ignores absent required columns, contradicting documented contract. Either move missing-column check validate_names(), update documentation. user runs check = "names" quick structural check get false sense safety. Files: R/validate.R (validate_names, validate_types_missing), documentation R/validate.R roxygen
code: schema <- sch_schema(x = sch_numeric(), y = sch_character()) sch_validate(schema, data.frame(x = 1), check = "names") # Passes silently — y is required but missing

what: 4. Custom type checks crash on NAs instead of producing a validation error
title: Code Review: schemar
text: Severity: Medium custom check function doesn’t handle NAs (common — package’s doc example doesn’t), (!type_fns[[tt$type]]$check(x, tt)) call validate_types_missing() can produce NA instead TRUE/FALSE, causes R throw bare simpleError (“missing value TRUE/FALSE needed”) rather structured sch_validation_error. validate loop wrap check call isTRUE() (already uses elsewhere) tryCatch, misbehaving check produces clean validation issue rather unhandled crash. doc example also fixed use na.rm = TRUE. Files: R/validate.R (validate_types_missing, ≈ line 164), R/schema.R (doc example)
code: even <- sch_custom(   name = "even",   check = function(x, type) is.integer(x) && all(x %% 2 == 0),   msg = function(type) "vector of even integers",   coerce = function(x, type) (as.integer(x) %/% 2) * 2 ) sch_validate(sch_schema(x = even), data.frame(x = c(2L, NA))) # Error: missing value where TRUE/FALSE needed  (unstructured crash)

what: 5. sch_inherits and sch_list_of descriptions are swapped
title: Code Review: schemar
text: roxygen @describeIn lines read: sch_inherits: “list-column whose elements satisfy inherits(_, class).” sch_list_of: “vector satisfying inherits(_, class).” actual check functions opposite: sch_inherits checks inherits(x, type$class) — .e. whole column. sch_list_of checks .list(x) && (vapply(x, inherits, …)) — .e. element. descriptions swapped match implementation. Files: R/schema.R (roxygen sch_inherits ≈ line 437, sch_list_of ≈ line 452)

what: 6. sch_nest(distinct=) is accepted but never validated
title: Code Review: schemar
text: sch_nest() accepts distinct argument stores attribute, validate_distinct() skips nest-typed columns (line 201: is_nest). parameter dead code. validate_distinct() actually carry validation nest-typed columns. Files: R/schema.R (sch_nest), R/validate.R (validate_distinct)

what: 8. distinct = TRUE with multiple NAs fails unexpectedly
title: Code Review: schemar
text: vctrs::vec_unique_count() counts NAs single unique value. column like c(1L, 2L, NA, NA) unique count 3 size 4, distinct check fails — even though missing = TRUE permits NAs. Users expecting SQL-style NULL ≠ NULL uniqueness semantics surprised. filter NAs uniqueness check. Files: R/validate.R (check_col_distinct)
code: schema <- sch_schema(x = sch_integer(distinct = TRUE, missing = TRUE)) sch_validate(schema, data.frame(x = c(1L, 2L, NA, NA))) # Error: duplicate values

what: 9. sch_list_of with NULL elements fails even when missing = TRUE
title: Code Review: schemar
text: list-columns, natural “NA” NULL. inherits(NULL, cls) returns FALSE, list containing NULLs always fails type check. missing-value check (anyNA) also won’t catch NULLs plain list. Files: R/schema.R (type_fnslistoflist_ofcheck)
code: schema <- sch_schema(x = sch_list_of(class = "data.frame", missing = TRUE)) df <- data.frame(dummy = 1:2) df$x <- list(data.frame(a = 1), NULL) sch_validate(schema, df)  # Error: wrong type

title: Build Structured Data Frame Subtypes
text: Provides developer-focused helper functions S3 classes ease creation structured subtypes data frames require certain columns types present, preserve special attributes common data frame manipulations.

title: Build Structured Data Frame Subtypes
text: can install development version schemar GitHub :

what: Define a structured data type — sch_schema
title: Define a structured data type — sch_schema
text: Defines structure single 'observation' structured data frame. column type restrictions may required optional. Schemas support nesting relationships.

title: Define a structured data type — sch_schema
code: sch_schema(..., .desc = NULL)  sch_others()  sch_nest(..., .keys = character(0), .desc = NULL)  sch_numeric(   desc = NULL,   bounds = c(-Inf, Inf),   closed = c(TRUE, TRUE),   missing = TRUE,   required = TRUE,   distinct = FALSE )  sch_integer(   desc = NULL,   bounds = c(-Inf, Inf),   closed = c(TRUE, TRUE),   missing = TRUE,   required = TRUE,   distinct = FALSE )  sch_logical(desc = NULL, missing = TRUE, required = TRUE, distinct = FALSE)  sch_character(desc = NULL, missing = TRUE, required = TRUE, distinct = FALSE)  sch_factor(   desc = NULL,   levels,   strict = TRUE,   missing = TRUE,   required = TRUE,   distinct = FALSE )  sch_date(   desc = NULL,   bounds = c(as.Date(-Inf), as.Date(Inf)),   closed = c(FALSE, FALSE),   missing = TRUE,   required = TRUE,   distinct = FALSE )  sch_datetime(   desc = NULL,   bounds = c(as.POSIXct(-Inf), as.POSIXct(Inf)),   closed = c(FALSE, FALSE),   missing = TRUE,   required = TRUE,   distinct = FALSE )  sch_inherits(   desc = NULL,   class,   missing = TRUE,   required = TRUE,   distinct = FALSE )  sch_list_of(   desc = NULL,   class,   missing = TRUE,   required = TRUE,   distinct = FALSE )  sch_custom(   name,   desc = NULL,   check,   msg,   coerce,   ...,   missing = TRUE,   required = TRUE,   distinct = FALSE )

title: Define a structured data type — sch_schema
text: ... Column specifications, form col_name = col_type pairs, col_type call column type constructor listed , sch_numeric(). Every type must kind vector, .e., vctrs::obj_is_vector() must return TRUE. columns must named, except sch_others(), described , unnamed sch_nest(), describes set columns logically nested within outer columns stored flat actual data frame. named sch_nest() describes columns stored nested data frame. special function sch_others() indicates preferred location columns explicitly mentioned schema. sch_others() appears, columns allowed. Trailing commas permitted. .keys character vector selecting one column names ... serve key columns nested group. desc, .desc description column consumers schema. type contraints described separately need included description.  example "age", description might "Age patient years", "Non-negative integer representing age patient years". bounds Length-two vector c(min, max) specifying allowed range values. closed Length-two logical vector specifying whether bounds closed (inclusive) open (exclusive). missing TRUE, column may contain missing values. Otherwise, missing values result error. required TRUE, column must present. FALSE, column optional. distinct TRUE, column must contain duplicate values (accounting nesting structure). levels character vector factor levels. strict TRUE, factors specified levels accepted. FALSE, character vectors specified levels also accepted. class character vector class names. name name custom type. check two-argument function checks whether object satisfies type. first argument object check, second full type specification. msg one-argument function generates descriptive message type passed type object . end period. coerce two-argument function attempts coerce object type. first argument object coerce, second full type specification.

title: Define a structured data type — sch_schema
text: object class sch_schema,

title: Define a structured data type — sch_schema
text: sch_others(): placeholder non-required columns schema. sch_nest(): set columns logically nested within outer schema. given name outer sch_schema(), columns stored nested data frame. unnamed, columns stored flat (adjacent outer columns). unique combinations rows defined .keys must repeat identically across groups defined outer columns. sch_numeric(): numeric vector optionally constrained within certain range. sch_integer(): integer vector optionally constrained within certain range. sch_logical(): logical vector. sch_character(): character vector. sch_factor(): factor specified levels. sch_date(): Date vector optionally constrained within certain range. sch_datetime(): POSIXct vector optionally constrained within certain range. sch_inherits(): list-column whose elements satisfy inherits(_, class). sch_list_of(): vector satisfying inherits(_, class). sch_custom(): custom type defined user-provided check, error, coercion functions. Additional named values stored along type specification may passed via ... available check, error, coercion functions.

title: Define a structured data type — sch_schema
code: sch_schema(     .desc = "Student data",     age = sch_integer("Age in years", bounds = c(0, 130)),     birthday = sch_date("Date of birth", required = FALSE),     height = sch_numeric(         "Height in inches",         bounds = c(0, 108),         closed = c(FALSE, TRUE)     ),     teacher = sch_factor(levels = c("Jones", "Smith", "Hernandez")),     enrolled = sch_logical(missing = FALSE),     sch_others() ) #> Student data #> A schema with 4 required columns: #>      age  Age in years: An integer vector with values in [0, 130]. #> birthday  Date of birth: A date vector. [Optional] #>   height  Height in inches: A numeric vector with values in (0, 108]. #>  teacher  A factor; one of Jones, Smith, or Hernandez. #> enrolled  A logical vector. No NAs allowed. #>      ...  Other columns  sch_schema(     .desc = "MCMC draws",     draw = sch_integer(         "Draw number",         bounds = c(1, Inf),         closed = c(TRUE, FALSE),         distinct = TRUE     ),     sch_nest(         param = sch_factor("Parameter name", levels = c("mu", "sigma", "log_lik")),         value = sch_numeric("Parameter value"),         .keys = "param"     ) ) #> MCMC draws #> A schema with 2 required columns: #> draw  Draw number: An integer vector with values in [1, Inf). [Distinct] #>       (flat): #>       param  Parameter name: A factor; one of mu, sigma, or log_lik. #>       value  Parameter value: A numeric vector.  sch_custom(    name = "even",    check = function(x, type) is.integer(x) && all(x %% 2 == 0),    msg = function(type) "vector of even integers",    coerce = function(x, type) (as.integer(x) %/% 2) * 2 ) #> A vector of even integers

what: Validate a data frame against a schema — sch_validate
title: Validate a data frame against a schema — sch_validate
text: Checks data frame conforms schema, validating column presence, types, missing values, uniqueness constraints, nesting structure.

title: Validate a data frame against a schema — sch_validate
code: sch_validate(   schema,   data,   check = c("names", "types", "distinct", "nesting"),   call = rlang::caller_env() )

title: Validate a data frame against a schema — sch_validate
text: schema schema object created sch_schema(). data data frame validate. check character vector specifying checks perform. default runs checks. Possible values: "names": check missing required columns unexpected extra columns. "types": check column types missing-value (NA) constraints. "distinct": check uniqueness constraints columns marked distinct = TRUE. Relatively expensive. "nesting": validate nested schema columns (flat named nests), including key consistency. Relatively expensive. call environment call used error reporting, passed rlang::abort(). Useful wrapping sch_validate() inside another function error points right place.

title: Validate a data frame against a schema — sch_validate
text: data, invisibly, validation succeeds. Otherwise, error class sch_validation_error raised formatted summary issues found.

title: Validate a data frame against a schema — sch_validate
code: # Basic validation: valid data passes silently schema <- sch_schema(     id = sch_integer(distinct = TRUE),     name = sch_character(missing = FALSE),     age = sch_numeric(required = FALSE) ) df <- data.frame(id = 1:3, name = c("Alice", "Bob", "Carol"), age = c(25, NA, 30)) sch_validate(schema, df)  # Invalid, throw validation errors if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # missing required columns sch_validate(schema, data.frame(id = 1:2))  # Validation error: type constrains sch_validate(schema, data.frame(id = c(1L, 1L), name = c("Alice", NA))) } # }
